text: Meeting deadlines is an important skill in both school and work. Good time management helps you plan ahead and avoid last-minute stress. Break large tasks into smaller steps and set mini-deadlines for each one. Prioritization is also key—focus on the most urgent or important tasks first, and leave less critical work for later. Sometimes, you may have too much to do, which is where scope management comes in. Learn to say no or ask for more time if your workload is unrealistic above all, communicate early and clearly. If you're struggling, tell your manager or teacher before the deadline. Honesty builds trust and can lead to helpful solutions.
